attacking_style: Attacks at kilometer zero or from established breakaways with 20-30km remaining, specializing in extreme-distance solo efforts (up to 200km). Thrives in constant-effort parcours rather than explosive climbing finishes.
team_tactics: Operates with minimal team support, self-sufficient breakaway specialist who creates gaps through relentless diesel pacing rather than coordinated team setups.
terrain_preference: Constant-effort terrain like tunnels and long solo runs where steady power output advantages diesel engine; exposed on classified climbs with high-pace finishes requiring explosive changes.
vulnerability: Cannot survive high pace on classified climb finishes despite holding climber jerseys. Lacks explosiveness when peloton or strong groups maintain threshold efforts on categorized ascents.
key_patterns: Kilometer zero attacks: launches extreme early breaks, surviving 200km solo (Tour) and pre-final attacks (Catalunya).
Diesel engine pacing: holds constant power where others fade, exploiting no-rest parcours and tunnel sections.
Breakaway specialist limitations: dropped when caught by peloton pace on categorized climbs despite polka dots.
Self-sufficient solo artist: maintains 23-27 second gaps through individual pacing against organized team pursuit pressure.
form_trajectory: neutral: 2019 Tour solo win demonstrates peak diesel capacity remains, but 2019 Paris-Nice drop confirms unchanged vulnerability to classified climb pace
